buggy_code:
for name, score in zip(scores, names):
    print(f'{name}: {score}')
task: Pair each name with its corresponding score (names list first, then scores)
error_location: line 1: zip order puts scores first, names second
clarification_query: In zip(scores, names), which variable receives the name and which the score in the loop?
clarification_options: name gets score and score gets name (order is swapped)
name gets name and score gets score
expected_user_response: name gets score and score gets name (order is swapped)
corrected_code:
for name, score in zip(names, scores):
    print(f'{name}: {score}')
explanation: zip(scores, names) yields (score, name) pairs. Unpacking to (name, score) assigns the score to name and vice versa.
